metadata.title:
CUNNINGHAM


HEARING IS


BEGUN HERE
SPOKANE MEN INTER-
ESTED In COLOSSAL


LAND CLAIM.

pages.raw_text:
With the $50, ooo, 900 Cunningham
coal claims in Alaska as the prize
at stake, the second sitting of the
government land office inquiry into
the alleged fraud in connection
with the entry of the claims began
here this afternoon in the federal
building.


The representatives of the gov
eminent will make no statement as
to the progress of the case in sup
port Of the charges, but those who
have followed the hearing closely
say that the government is making
good and that it will be difficult
task for the entrymen to finally
pass muster on the claims.


MAY LAST TEN DAYS.


Attorney John P. Gray of Wal
lace, Idaho, one of the associate
counsel for the claimants, today
said that the entrymen were salts
fled with the progress of the hear
ing and felt that the facts would
finally justify the government in is
suing the required patent.


The Spokane hearing may con-
tinue week or 10 days, owing
largely to the speed to be attained
in the examination of Witnesses.
From here the hearing will adjourn
to Wallace, Idaho. and thence to
Minneapolis and Cleveland. In the
hearing in Spokane some 12 or Is
entrymen living in Spokane and the
Inland Empire will be called. and
the duration of the hearing will be
determined by the time taken In
the examination of each witness.
